hypothesis: Systems-optimizer salvage becomes safer if the runner is forced to execute the systems-optimizer self-verifier before a ratchet is considered replayable or recoverable, while promotion stays bound to the scope-local systems-optimizer bundle.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `runner-policy.json` could omit `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` from the systems-optimizer salvage command groups, so recovery could advance without checking backlog/state contract drift. After the ratchet, the runner-policy declares that self-verifier as a required systems-optimizer command and `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` fails if it disappears, while the scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if systems-optimizer salvage requires `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs`, the verifier enforces that requirement, and the scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer recovery must self-verify its own state and backlog contract before replay or salvage, and that safety check has to promote through the scope-local bundle rather than stale-truth-sensitive global health gates.
